title: inula × rigida var. hispida
intro: Inula × rigida var. hispida, also known as the Golden Ragwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Inula × rigida var. hispida include reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms associated with colds, fever, and digestive issues. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
